reasoning:
MMTY (Treasury Yield: Money Market <100M cap) historically tracks the Fed Funds target rate very closely due to its formulaic calculation as a rate cap for undercapitalized institutions. The series remained completely flat at 4.33% from Jan to Sep 2025, before dropping steadily in subsequent months corresponding perfectly to Fed rate cuts (~24bp drop after a 50bp cut due to calculation specifics, then another ~23bp drop to 3.86% in Nov, then a tiny bounce to 3.89% on Dec 1). 

Crucially, the FOMC delivered a 25bp rate cut in mid-December 2025. The December 1 reading of 3.89% almost certainly relies on data from before this cut. Thus, the next monthly update in mid-January 2026 (for the Jan 20 target) is extremely likely to reflect the new lower overnight rate, dropping the MMTY measure towards the ~3.65% range. For MMTY to rebound above its current 3.89% value by March 2026, the FOMC would need to deliver a sudden rate hike shortly after its December cut. While the December dot plot may be 'more hawkish' (i.e. fewer cuts expected in 2026), an outright short-term hike is extremely unlikely. Therefore, the series is highly expected to be strictly lower on both resolution dates than its December 2025 baseline.
